applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile
applied_optimizations.description: Applied torch.compile(mode='default') to the forward pass, achieving ~1.5x speedup from 11.7ms to 7.31ms

attempted_but_failed.name: F.scaled_dot_product_attention with separate projections
attempted_but_failed.reason: Output mismatch - different weight initialization compared to nn.MultiheadAttention fused in_proj_weight

attempted_but_failed.name: Custom Triton attention kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Complexity and initialization mismatch - reverted to simpler torch.compile approach

notes: torch.compile(mode='default') alone provides significant speedup (~1.5x) for this attention problem
The autotuner selected rocBLAS/bmm kernels over Triton MM kernels for most operations
Setting float32 matmul precision to 'high' enables TF32 tensor cores on supported hardware
max-autotune mode was tried but resulted in slower performance (14.2ms) with high variance due to runtime kernel search overhead
Further optimization may require custom Triton kernels for layer norm or attention, or trying different inductor configs

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trials 2, 3, and 4 all produced zero output — the agent is getting stuck planning without executing. Providing complete copy-paste code and exact shell commands to eliminate any ambiguity.

global_notes: Stage 1 complete: baseline established at 11.7ms, optimized to 7.31ms (1.50x speedup, SCORE: 65.0)
Key optimization: torch.compile(mode='default') with float32 matmul precision set to 'high'
Attempted max-autotune mode but it was slower (14.2ms vs 7.31ms) due to runtime overhead
Future work: Could explore custom Triton kernels for layer norm, kernel fusion, or different inductor configs
